symptom: Outdoor unit does not energize when thermostat calls for cooling. Indoor blower runs. 24V control voltage present at Y terminal but compressor and fan stay off.
diagnosis: Pitted, stuck, or failed 24V contactor. Common on Trane XR14 after 5-8 years in humid climates.
procedure: Kill power at the disconnect and verify with a tester.
Open the control compartment on the condenser.
Visually inspect the contactor — pitting, burn marks, or welded contacts are failure signs.
Check the 24V coil for continuity (should read ~10-20 ohms). Open coil = replace.
With power restored, energize the thermostat call for cool and listen for a definitive 'click' at the contactor. No click with 24V present at the coil = failed contactor.
Kill power again. Label and remove line-voltage leads (L1, L2) and low-voltage leads.
Swap in CTR02266 (2-pole, 30A, 24V coil). Reconnect leads in the same positions.
Restore power, test cooling cycle. Confirm contactor pulls in cleanly and compressor starts.
safety_notes: Line voltage is present on the contactor's top lugs even with the disconnect pulled if the unit is still wired to a live breaker — check BOTH.
Never bypass a contactor by jumpering L1 to T1 as a diagnostic — hazardous and can damage the compressor.
